core_mechanism: nearest-neighbor analog forecasting via a tunable linear (convex) blend: the next state is predicted as a weighted interpolation between the current state and the temporal successor of its closest match in a stored reference library, yielding a low-parameter iterated map whose blend weight traces a one-parameter family from pure successor-copying to bounded chaotic divergence
brief_reason: The abstract states DynaBase forecasts through a linear blend of the current latent state and the nearest in-context neighbor and its temporal successor, and that varying this produces a 1-parameter family of maps spanning context-parroting at one end and bounded chaos at the other.
